denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/22/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100942361          Group #: GRP-1119
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-120324
Claim #: CLM-2026-194308
Date of Service (requested): 05/02/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 05/04/2026 for the requested service.: Catheter ablation for symptomatic AFib
Diagnosis (as submitted): [REDACTED]
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM139 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM139 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Catheter ablation for symptomatic AFib is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100942361) and reference PA-2026-120324. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (I48.0). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Catheter ablation for symptomatic AFib recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed dofetilide and sotalol with Holter-documented burden 18%; CHA2DS2-VASc 2. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
